task_description: Search for books by Mark Twain and mark the first result as read.

caption.observation: Cropped image shows The zoomed crop shows the Goodreads header search field with the placeholder text "Search books," and a semitransparent red X centered on that input box. In the full screen, a Goodreads search results page is open in Chrome, with the Goodreads logo at top left, navigation links like Home, My Books, Browse, and Community, and a list of book search results below. The target is a top-area UI control: the small search box in the site header.
caption.action: Click the Goodreads header search box labeled "Search books."
caption.expectation: The search field should become active and place a text cursor inside it, ready for typing.

caption.observation: Cropped image shows The zoomed area is the Goodreads header search field near the top center of the page. The text box contains the partially entered query "mark twai" and an autocomplete panel is open beneath it, listing book suggestions such as "The Adventures of Huckleberry Finn," "The Adventures of Tom Sawyer," "The Prince and the Pauper," and "A Connecticut Yankee in King Arthur's Court," with author names shown below. In the full screen, this is a Goodreads search results page with the Goodreads logo at top left, navigation links like Home, My Books, Browse, and Community, and book results currently shown for a different query in the main content area. The typing is going into the active site search box and appears to be replacing the earlier search term rather than appending to the old results query.
caption.action: Type "MARK TWAIN" into the active Goodreads search field.
caption.expectation: The search text in the header box should update to MARK TWAIN, and the autocomplete suggestions should refresh to match that author name.

caption.observation: Cropped image shows The cropped image shows a semitransparent red X centered on the small black magnifying-glass icon at the right edge of the Goodreads header search bar. Just to its right are the text links "Sign In" and "Join." In the full-screen view, the Goodreads website is open in Chrome, the search field contains "mark twain," and an autocomplete dropdown is visible with book and author suggestions beneath the search bar. The target is a top-area UI control: the search submit icon.
caption.action: Click the magnifying-glass search icon at the right end of the Goodreads search bar.
caption.expectation: The autocomplete dropdown should close and Goodreads should load search results for "mark twain."

caption.observation: Cropped image shows The cropped image shows a semitransparent red X centered on the small downward-pointing arrow segment at the right edge of the green "Want to Read" button for the first result, "The Adventures of Huckleberry Finn." A dropdown menu is already open directly beneath that button, showing reading-status options including "Want to Read," "Currently Reading," "Read," and "Did Not Finish." In the full-screen view, this is the Goodreads search results page for "mark twain," with book results listed in the main column and related shelves on the right. The target is an object-level control within the first book result card: the status dropdown toggle on the green shelf button.
caption.action: Click the small dropdown-arrow area on the green "Want to Read" button for the first book result.
caption.expectation: The reading-status menu should remain open or refresh focus on that book's shelf options, allowing a status choice to be selected.

caption.observation: Cropped image shows The cropped image shows a semitransparent red X centered on the right side of the green "Want to Read" button for the first result, "The Adventures of Huckleberry Finn," specifically over the small downward-pointing dropdown-arrow segment. Immediately to the right is a beige "Get a copy" button. In the full-screen view, this is the Goodreads search results page for "mark twain," with book results listed in the main column and related shelves on the right. The target is a UI control within the first book result card, not a blank area.
caption.action: Click the small dropdown-arrow area on the green "Want to Read" button for the first book result.
caption.expectation: A reading-status menu should open beneath or near that button, showing shelf/status options for the selected book.

caption.observation: Cropped image shows The cropped image shows a semitransparent red X centered on the dropdown menu item labeled "Read" within the open reading-status menu beneath the green "Want to Read" button for the first book, "The Adventures of Huckleberry Finn." The menu also shows other options including "Want to Read," "Currently Reading," and "Did Not Finish." In the full-screen view, this is the Goodreads search results page for "mark twain," and the open menu belongs to the first search result card. The target is a menu control option, not a blank area.
caption.action: Click the "Read" option in the open reading-status dropdown menu for the first book result.
caption.expectation: The menu should close and the book's shelf status should update to indicate it is marked as read.
